授業目的: 1. To develop students’ English proficiency through the study of academic topics in the field of sociology.2. To build students’ confidence in using English as a communicative tool.3. To help students improve their listening and note-taking skills through extensive lecture-listening activities. 4. To introduce students to significant vocabulary words relevant to a wide range of academic topics. 5. To prepare students to actively take part in discussions, teaching them the fundamental skills for agreeing and disagreeing with others’ opinions.
到達目標: 1. Students will develop their academic skills. 2. Students will become more confident and effective communicators.3. Students will strengthen their listening comprehension, note-taking and discussion skills.4. Students will acquire new vocabulary that will enhance their academic achievement.
授業方法: 1. This course will be conducted in English only and English will be the means of communication between the students and the instructor.  (Students will also be expected to communicate with one another in English.)2. Students are encouraged to actively participate in class discussions, both independently and collaboratively, in order to enhance their competence and confidence in English and also to create a better learning environment for all.

トピック.授業外学習2: One to two hours of homework can be expected each week. Students will be asked to watch course materials online, mostly lecture videos, throughout the semester.

教科書/Required texts0: Contemporary Topics Introductory (Supplementary materials will be provided by the instructor whenever needed.)Students are encouraged to bring a dictionary to class.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Students will independently evaluate the instructor and the course at the end of the semester.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Students’ final grades will derive from a combination of assignments, activities, presentations, mini tests, etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note 1. Active participation is extremely important and counts as a significant part of students’ evaluation marks.  2. Attendance is crucial to students’ success in this course. The instructor will carefully mark students’ attendance as present and on time, late, or absent. Specific rules regarding attendance are as follows: 2a) Only students who are present at the time the class begins will receive full attendance marks. 2b) Any student walking into the classroom after the class has begun will be marked late. The instructor will take this into account when giving students a participation grade.2c) Being 15 to 29 minutes late will equal half an absence (0.5). 2d) Each time students are late 30 minutes or more, they will be marked absent. However, they are still encouraged to attend.                2e) No more than three absences will be permitted in this course. Any student who is absent more than three                times will automatically fail this course.
備考Note0: In principle, the use of smartphones, tablets, and other electronic devices in the classroom is forbidden. Students must store them in their bags throughout the entire duration of each class.  In the case of a project in which internet connection is necessary, the instructor may instruct students to use their electronic devices, but unless such a situation is specifically and clearly suggested by the instructor, their use will be strictly prohibited.

成績評価備考:
備考　Note
1. Active participation is extremely important and counts as a significant part of students’ evaluation marks.  
2. Attendance is crucial to students’ success in this course. The instructor will carefully mark students’ attendance as present and on time, late, or absent. Specific rules regarding attendance are as follows: 
2a) Only students who are present at the time the class begins will receive full attendance marks. 
2b) Any student walking into the classroom after the class has begun will be marked late. The instructor will take this into account when giving students a participation grade.
2c) Being 15 to 29 minutes late will equal half an absence (0.5). 
2d) Each time students are late 30 minutes or more, they will be marked absent. However, they are still encouraged to attend.  
              2e) No more than three absences will be permitted in this course. Any student who is absent more than three 
               times will automatically fail this course.
